description:
You are working in the `guru-pipeline` Python repo. The corpus acquisition stage (`scripts/acquire.py` reading `sources/manifest.toml`) is already working and producing cleaned plaintext in `raw/{tradition}/{text_id}.txt`. The chunking stage (`scripts/chunk.py`) currently has per-text chunking configs that are becoming unwieldy as the corpus grows. The goal is to refactor chunking around **three reusable strategy templates** so that adding a new source becomes a small config file rather than bespoke code. Each source in `sources/manifest.toml` picks a strategy by name and supplies parameters. The strategy templates are:
1. **`regex-section-split`** — single-page texts with consistent section markers (e.g. `^YASNA [IVXLCDM]+`, `^\s*\d+\.\s*$`, `(?:^|
)\(?(\d+)\)\s+`). Splits one raw file into N chunks by regex boundary.
2. **`page-as-chunk`** — multi-page books where each raw file represents one natural chunk. Each page becomes one chunk (with optional sub-splitting on paragraph boundaries if it exceeds the token budget).
3. **`paragraph-group`** — prose without obvious section markers. Groups N consecutive paragraphs per chunk within the token budget, splitting on blank lines.
All three strategies must enforce a maximum token count (default 800) and preserve citation metadata per chunk.
